page.title: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis: 168 alterations and 305 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: A systematic review and meta-analysis off the effects of cannabinoids in Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis murine models.
In this meta-analysis, there was an observed increased risk of amytrophic lateral sclerosis in workers occupationally exposed to extremely low frequency electromagnetic fields.
There is an association between occupational exposure to power frequency electromagnetic fields and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
"Consumption of foods high inω-3 PUFAs may help prevent or delay the onset of ALS."
A high intake of poluunsaturated fatty acids and vitamin E is associated with a 50-60% decreased risk of developing ALS.
A higher intake of food rich in antioxidants such as fruit and vegetables confer protection against the development of ALS.
A majority of patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis had signs of intestinal inflammation.
Acupuncture treatment appears to have value on respiratory physiology parameters in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis patients: a pilot study.
An ALS-specific meditation programme is beneficial for the quality of life and psychological well-being of people with ALS.
Antioxidants, carotenes, fruits, and vegetables were associated with higher ALS function.
Autologous bone marrow-derived stem cell therapy is safe and feasible in patients of ALS.
Capsaicin may improve swallowing impairment in patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
Cigarette smoking is associated with increased risk for ALS.
Coenzyme Q10 levels as high as 3,000 mg a day are safe and well-tolerated in ALS patients.
Diaphragm training has value in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
Efficacy of hypnosis-based treatment in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis: a pilot study.
Efficacy of mecasin for treatment of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
Enhanced acute muscle activation in ALS patients following liposomal curcumin, resveratrol, and dutasteride administration.

Existential well-being, as well as spirituality issues, perceived by SALS patients seems to be directly related with quality of life, severity of mood disturbance and burden experienced by their caregivers.
Free testosterone is significantly decreased in ALS, indicating that supplementation with testosterone may have a therapeutic role in ALS treatment.
Glutamate levels may contribute to the pathogenesis of ALS.
High homocysteine levels are associated with neurodegenerative conditions such as Parkinson's disease, ALS and progressive supranuclear palsy.
High homocysteine levels may be associated with the pathogenesis of neurological disorders.
High plasma concentrations of organic pollutants negatively impact survival in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
In ALS patients, we report that a severe vitamin D deficiency accelerates the rate of decline and were associated with a marked shorter life expectancy.
It is possible that lesions suggestive of Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis may be attributed to celiac disease.
JWSJZ decoction may be a safe treatment for ALS, and may have delayed the development of ALS.
Lead exposure is a risk factor for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
Methylcobalamin may prevent muscle wasting in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
Occupational exposures to agricultural chemicals is associated with increased risk for ALS.
Pesticide exposure is associated with an increased risk of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
Progesterone levels correlate positively with survival and prognosis in ALS patients.
Selenium, glutathione, copper/zinc and superoxide dismutase may have therapeutic value in ALS.
Serum vitamin E levels have a protective effect on ALS risk.
Significant neurological improvement in two patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis after 4 weeks of treatment with acupuncture injection point therapy using enercel.
Statin drug use may be linked to neuromuscular diseases and an amyotrophic lateral sclerosis-like syndrome.
Statin drugs may cause ALS-like neurodegeneration in susceptible individuals.
Suboptimal vitamin D levels are frequently found in ALS patients (81%).
There is an association between blood lead and the risk of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
There is increased mitochondrial oxidative damage in patients with sporadic amyotrophic lateral sclerosis

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Essentially zero — uniformly progressive, fatal within 2-5 years median; <10% survive >10 years; spontaneous prolonged stabilisation in <1% (SOD1 heterozygous late-onset)
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Riluzole: 3-month median survival extension (modest); edaravone (Radicava): slows functional decline by 33% at 24 weeks in a specific ALS subgroup (ALSFRS-R); AMX0035 (sodium phenylbutyrate + taurursodiol): 6.5-month OS extension (CENTAUR trial — modest); tofersen (SOD1-ALS): 38% reduction in neurofilament light (biomarker); 2-year mortality benefit for SOD1-ALS
remission.gap_size: Very large — ALS has no effective treatment; existing drugs provide modest or biomarker-based benefit; curative approaches are distant
remission.barrier_detail: ALS is arguably the most therapeutically challenging neurodegenerative disease; 25+ Phase 3 trials have failed; tofersen (SOD1-targeting antisense oligonucleotide) represents the first mechanism-targeted approach and the most promising signal yet; multigenicity and sporadic forms make uniform treatment impossible
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — tofersen (Qalsody) FDA approved for SOD1-ALS based on neurofilament biomarker; AMX0035 (Relyvrio) approved 2022 — subsequently voluntary market withdrawal 2024 after Phase 3 PHOENIX trial failure; edaravone 2017; riluzole 1995
remission.outdated_treatments: High-dose antioxidant cocktails (vitamin E, CoQ10 — multiple large trials negative); minocycline (neuroprotective, Phase 3 failed — possible harm); celecoxib + minocycline combinations (negative); lithium (Phase 3 failed despite positive pilot); stem cell treatments without evidence-based protocols
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Multidisciplinary ALS clinic care (demonstrated 7-month OS advantage vs standard neurology follow-up — one of the strongest intervention effects in ALS); NIV (non-invasive ventilation) extending survival by 7-11 months; percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) for nutrition; exercise — gentle, not strenuous
remission.frontier_signal: Tofersen (SOD1-targeting ASO — first mechanism-targeted ALS drug; extending to pre-symptomatic prevention in SOD1 mutation carriers); C9orf72-targeting ASOs; FUS-targeting approaches; cell therapy (NurOwn stem cells — Phase 3 failed US); CRISPR base editing for ALS-causing mutations; stem-cell derived astrocyte replacement
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
